channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Philips Monitors is now offering a whopping 5-year warranty on some of its displays, including a gorgeous KVM-enabled business monitor
item.description: Philips Monitors extends sustainability efforts with a five-year warranty on TCO Certified, generation 10 displays across Europe.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Beterbiev vs Bivol 2 LIVE: Fight stream, live updates, cheapest PPV deals, how to watch light-heavyweight title rematch
item.description: The Russians meet again in Saudi Arabia – here are the best streams, the cheapest prices and how to watch Artur Beterbiev vs Dmitry Bivol live online.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sandisk's revolutionary new memory promises DRAM-like performance, 4X capacity at half the price
item.description: Sandisk's upcoming 3D Matrix Memory promises DRAM-like performance at four times the capacity and half the cost.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Rich in cash, Japan automaker Toyota builds a city to test futuristic mobility
item.description: Woven City near Mount Fuji is where Japanese automaker Toyota plans to test everyday living with robotics, artificial intelligence and autonomous zero-emissions transportation.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Handmaid's Tale season 6: everything we know so far about the hit Hulu show’s return
item.description: The Handmaid's Tale season 6 will be available to stream on Hulu in April 2025 – here's everything we know about the dystopian drama's return, while we wait.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Majority of Americans dislike Musk, Zuckerberg: Poll
item.description: A Pew Research Center poll shows 54% of Americans have an "unfavorable" view of Musk, while 67% have an "unfavorable" view of Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ dropping immigration case against Musk's SpaceX
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) said Thursday it is planning to drop its lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceX, which alleged the company discriminated against refugees and asylees during the hiring process.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: AMD RX 9070 GPU spec and benchmark rumors cast fresh doubt on power usage and performance – but I wouldn’t worry about the latter
item.description: Rumors continue to fly about AMD’s RDNA 4 GPUs, and here we’ve got news on power usage, and a somewhat shaky-sounding story with performance.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Rivian records first gross profit as EV policy risks loom
item.description: The California-based company's fourth quarter result easily topped the $32 million expected by Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: North Korean hackers are posing as software development recruiters to target freelancers
item.description: Freelance software developers are being hit with infostealing malware to fund the North Korean regime, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: HP is apparently forcing customer support callers to wait 15 minutes before talking to anyone
item.description: HP wants customers to self-service their problems so much it’s reportedly adding a 15-minute wait to calls.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: No, Apple and Meta aren’t making humanoid robots
item.description: Tech press-landi and the tech blogosphere were abuzz last week with news that both Apple, the company that makes the iPhone, and Meta, the company that makes everybody on Facebook confront a daily flood of horrible AI-generated slop, are making full-size, humanoid robots.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI humanoid robots inch their way toward the workforce
item.description: AI-based humanoid robots could soon start becoming a workforce reality, thanks to parallel advances in generative AI and electromechanical components and mechatronics, enabling robots to have reasoning skills as well as physical abilities that exceed their predecessors.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Netflix to invest $1 billion in Mexico production over next 4 years
item.description: The chief executive of streaming giant Netflix on Thursday announced a $1 billion investment to produce some 20 films and TV series in Mexico annually over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: INE Security’s Cybersecurity and IT Training Enhances Career Stability in Tech
item.description: 2025 marks a time of unprecedented volatility in the technology job market. On one hand, dependence on technology is soaring. The growth of AI and machine learning is propelling a surge in new technologies, tactics, and ideas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Deno update brings OpenTelemetry integration
item.description: Deno Land has released Deno 2.2, a new version of the Deno runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript that features built-in OpenTelemetry for monitoring logs, metrics, and traces. The new Deno release also features Linter updates that include a new plugin system and new rules for React and Preact users.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk calls former International Space Station commander 'idiot' in exchange over astronauts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called a former commander of the International Space Station an “idiot” on Thursday after he pushed back on Musk’s claim that the Biden administration “left” two astronauts in space “for political reasons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Perplexity releases a censorship-free variant of Deepseek R1
item.description: Perplexity is releasing its model R1 1776, a version of Deepseek R1 with open model weights that has been post-trained to remove China’s censorship and provide more unbiased, accurate answers, according to Perplexity co-founder and CEO Aravind Srinivas. He wrote about the move on LinkedIn.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Huge data leak exposes 14 million customer shipping records
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Hipshipper, which is used by eBay, Shopify and Amazon sellers, exposed millions of shipping labels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Charred EV batteries from California wildfires spark concern about toxic waste contaminating drinking water
item.description: A water agency in charge of ground water for 2 million L.A. County residents has several concerns about hazardous waste processing from the Eaton fire.
item.dc:creator: Steve Scauzillo, The Mercury News

item.title: DOGE efforts to access private data spark sharp pushback
item.description: Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) efforts to tap into databases across the government is facing broad pushback from a variety of groups sounding the alarm over the privacy and security risks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk finally reveals 'why Memphis' for xAI supercomputer
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Months after xAI put its operations in Memphis, Elon Musk finally announced why he chose the Bluff City to host the world's largest artificial intelligence supercomputer.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Fortinet speeds threat detection with improved FortiAnalyzer
item.description: Fortinet has reinforced its FortiAnalyzer security analytics and log management platform with capabilities that enhance enterprise threat detection and automate incident response. FortiAnalyzer securely collects logs from Fortinet devices such as FortiGate firewalls and endpoint security agents.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don't let AI phantom hackers drain your bank account
item.description: Attackers use caller ID spoofing and AI voices to steal money. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides seven ways to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: With its legacy of innovation in GPU technology, Nvidia has become a dominant force in the AI market. Nvidia’s partners read like a technology who’s who list – e.g.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
